one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG764-recommended free cardiac rehabilitation platform from a UK heart failure charity — uniquely reaching underserved populations with 63% women and one-third from the most deprived English cities, with peer-to-peer community support.
why_it_matters_locally: Pumping Marvellous addresses the well-documented gender and deprivation disparities in cardiac rehabilitation uptake. With 63% women (reversing the typical gender bias in CR) and one-third of registrants from the top 20 most deprived English cities, it demonstrates an ability to reach populations that conventional CR programmes consistently fail to engage. The platform is entirely free, funded by educational grants.
target_problem_statement: Severe underrepresentation of women and deprived communities in cardiac rehabilitation, low HF-specific CR provision, and cost barriers to digital CR adoption.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Conditionally recommended — moved from 'research only' (Aug 2025 draft) to recommended (Dec 2025 final)

evidence_summary: Service evaluation: Sankaranarayanan et al. (2025, medRxiv preprint), n=673 registrants (Aug 2024 - Jul 2025). 63% women, one-third from top 20 most deprived English cities. Referral sources: ~30% from HF/CR teams, 29% via social media, ~25% from PMF groups, 4% from GPs. NICE noted clinical evidence for the platform is uncertain, but clinical and financial risk judged low. Designed using evidence-based NHS programmes for heart failure.

case_studies.title: Early insights from Pumping Marvellous home-based digital CR for heart failure
case_studies.outcome: 673 registrants in first year. 63% women (reversing typical CR gender disparity). One-third from top 20 most deprived English cities. Referrals from HF/CR teams (30%), social media (29%), PMF groups (25%), GPs (4%).
case_studies.caveat: medRxiv preprint — not yet peer-reviewed. Service evaluation, not an RCT. Clinical evidence for the platform itself is uncertain per NICE.

live_sites: UK-wide, free access. 673 registrants in first year (launched August 2024).
patients_covered_note: 673 registrants in first year (Aug 2024 - Jul 2025). Platform is free and open access — not contracted to specific NHS trusts.
dtac_note: Information not available. DTAC approval is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation.
device_class: Web-based platform. Registered UK charity, not a commercial medical device company.
cyber_notes: Registered UK charity. Clinical Advisory Board oversees the platform. Confirm security certifications.
gdpr_note: Confirm data handling and privacy terms with Pumping Marvellous Foundation.
target_patients: Adults with heart failure, particularly those underserved by conventional CR: women, patients from deprived areas, those unwilling or unable to attend centre-based CR.
exclusions: NICE requires clinical assessment before offering the platform, even though self-referral is possible.
onboarding_detail: Free to use. Self-referral possible alongside clinical referral. Free 8-week programme with accompanying educational booklet at no cost. Booklets orderable by HF/CR teams.
supplier_wrap: Structured exercise programmes (low and medium function levels), educational material, psychosocial support through peer-to-peer online community.
local_wraparound_detail: Very low local effort. Platform is free and self-referral is supported. HF/CR teams can order free booklets and refer patients. NICE requires initial clinical assessment.
training_note: No specific training required. Booklets and resources available for NHS teams at no cost.
monitoring_model: Self-management with peer-to-peer community support. No clinician dashboard described.
monitoring_note: Self-management platform. Not a monitored clinical service. Peer-to-peer community support.
escalation_note: Standard NHS escalation routes. Platform is not a clinical service. Clinical Advisory Board oversees content.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a clinical service or monitored platform. Peer support community only.
implementation_prerequisites: Clinical assessment before offering the platform (NICE requirement)
indicative_price_text: Free to patients and NHS teams. Funded by educational grant from Pharmacosmos UK.
service_wrap_note: Free platform. No procurement or licensing cost. Booklets available at no charge via NHS team order forms.
contract_note: No procurement or licensing cost. Free to use.
expected_benefit_note: NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. Key benefit is reaching underserved populations (women, deprived areas) who do not attend conventional CR.
tariff_considerations: No platform cost. Commissioner investment is limited to clinical assessment capacity and pathway integration.
roi_note: Free platform. ROI is driven by reaching patients who would not otherwise access CR, with associated secondary prevention benefits.
nhse_125k_note: Not applicable — cardiac rehabilitation product. Platform is already free.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Clinical assessment pathway for patients before offering the platform. Awareness among HF/CR teams.
procurement_notes: No procurement required. Free platform. Order booklets via NHS team forms.

technical_integrations.device_integration: No device integration. Web-based, accessible via any browser.

context_of_use.population: Adults with heart failure, particularly women and patients from deprived areas
context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Structured exercise (low and medium function), education, psychosocial support, peer-to-peer community
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Minimal — clinical assessment required before offering, but platform is self-directed with peer support
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG764 — conditionally recommended during 3-year evidence generation period

demo_notes: Platform is freely accessible. Visit the website to explore.
source_summary: NICE HTG764, Sankaranarayanan et al. 2025 (medRxiv preprint), pumpingmarvellous.org.
confidence_note: Clinical evidence for the platform is uncertain per NICE. Designed using evidence-based NHS programmes. Uniquely reaches underserved populations. DTAC approval must be obtained. Verify all details with Pumping Marvellous Foundation.
